name: The Mortgage Outlet
description_short: Family-owned mortgage broker and lender offering home purchase, refinance, and home equity loans across most U.S. states with specialized programs for tech workers, self-employed borrowers, and first-time homebuyers.
description_long:
The Mortgage Outlet is a family-owned mortgage broker and lender that provides residential home financing solutions nationwide. The company operates with a focus on serving diverse borrower profiles through specialized loan programs rather than traditional one-size-fits-all lending. They are licensed to originate loans in California, Colorado, Oregon, and Washington, and can broker loans in most other U.S. states, territories, and protectorates.

The company's core offerings include home purchase loans, refinance mortgages, and home equity loans. What distinguishes The Mortgage Outlet is their development of niche programs: the RSU Income Loan Program specifically for tech employees that counts Restricted Stock Units as qualifying income; the Bank Statement Loan Program for self-employed borrowers who can document income through 12 months of bank statements rather than traditional tax returns; and the High Cost Area Purchase Program offering agency credits for interest rate buydowns to first-time homebuyers. They also offer traditional home equity loans and cash-out refinances for debt consolidation, home improvements, education expenses, and general cash needs.

The company emphasizes three core value propositions: speed (claiming closings in as few as 10 days), transparency (no junk fees like application or loan processing fees), and personal service (positioning themselves as "real people" with "real support"). Their marketing emphasizes the contrast between their approach and larger, more impersonal lenders.

As a broker and lender hybrid, The Mortgage Outlet can provide wider access than direct lenders alone, though borrowers should verify specific program availability in their state since licensing is limited to four states while brokerage services span most of the country. The specialized programs are advantageous for non-traditional borrowers, but program details regarding rates, terms, and actual closing timelines are not transparently disclosed on the website.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: RSU Income Loan Program specifically designed for tech employees, allowing stock units to count as qualifying income
Bank Statement Loan Program for self-employed borrowers eliminating need for tax returns and W-2s
No junk fees policy—explicitly states no application or loan processing fees
Fast closings—claims ability to close loans in as few as 10 days
High Cost Area Purchase Program with agency credits for interest rate buydowns targeting first-time homebuyers
Home equity loans and cash-out refinances for multiple use cases (debt consolidation, home improvements, education)
Operates across all 50 states plus territories, providing broad geographic reach
cons: Limited direct lending licenses—only California, Colorado, Oregon, and Washington; other states rely on brokerage partnerships which may restrict program availability
No rate, terms, or APR information disclosed on website—borrowers cannot comparison shop before applying
Closing timeline claims of '10 days' are unverified marketing claims without documented evidence or average timelines
No clear disclosure of which specialized programs are available in which states or credit score requirements
Family-owned positioning lacks third-party certifications, ratings, or customer review data visible on site
best_for: Tech workers and startup employees with Restricted Stock Units seeking to qualify for mortgages
Self-employed borrowers, freelancers, and business owners who lack traditional tax return documentation
First-time homebuyers in high-cost real estate markets seeking rate buydown assistance
diagnosis: Best for non-traditional borrowers—particularly tech workers with RSU compensation and self-employed individuals—who value streamlined underwriting and transparent fee structures. Main caveat: actual loan terms, rates, and program availability by state are not disclosed online, requiring direct contact with the company to understand costs and eligibility, and fast closing claims should be verified independently rather than assumed.
services: RSU Income Loan Program for tech employees
Bank Statement Loan Program for self-employed borrowers
High Cost Area Purchase Program with rate buydown credits
Mortgage brokerage services across most U.S. states
Direct mortgage lending in CA, CO, OR, WA
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 200 N Almaden Blvd Suite 200, San Jose, CA 95110
